gloss_en: How are you today? Are you well?
notes: wāš rāk greeting (PADIC-ALGIERS attests rāk); mlīḥ 'well' (Algiers hallmark); Algiers urban phonology/lexicon per (boucherit2002)

sentence: اَلْجَوّ شْبَاب الْيُوم وَالسّمَا صَافْيَة فِي الْعَاصِمَة.
raw: الجو شباب اليوم والسما صافية في العاصمة.
ipa: alˈʒaww ˈʃbaːb alˈjuːm ˈwassmaː ˈsˤɑːfja ˈfiː lˈʕɑːsˤima
gloss_en: The weather is beautiful today and the sky is clear in the capital.
notes: šbāb 'beautiful' (Algiers lexeme); ج=/ʒ/ (grand_henry1972); sun assimilation as-s

sentence: مَا نْحَبّْش قَهْوَة، نْحَبّ أَتَاي سْخُون.
raw: ما نحبش قهوة، نحب أتاي سخون.
ipa: ˈmaː ˈnħabbʃ ˈqahwa ˈnħabb ʔaˈtaːj ˈsxuːn
gloss_en: I don't want coffee, I like hot tea.
notes: ma-...-š circumfix + n- 1sg (Maghrebi, grand_henry1972); geminate بّ; cluster sx-

sentence: شْرِيت تْلَاتَة كِيلُو بَطَاطَا مِن السُّوق بِمِيَة دِينَار.
raw: شريت تلاتة كيلو بطاطا من السوق بمية دينار.
ipa: ˈʃriːt ˈtlaːta ˈkiːluː bɑˈtˤɑːtˤɑː ˈmin asˈsuːq ˈbimija diːˈnaːr
gloss_en: I bought three kilos of potatoes from the market for a hundred dinars.
notes: ث→/t/ tlāta (Algiers stop reflexes, grand_henry1972); ق=/q/ sūq; cluster šr-

sentence: هَادَاك الرَّاجِل يِخْدِم فِي الظّهْر بْزَّاف.
raw: هاداك الراجل يخدم في الظهر بزاف.
ipa: haːˈdaːk arˈraːʒil ˈjixdim ˈfiː ˈɑdˤdˤhr ˈbzzaːf
gloss_en: That man works a lot at noon.
notes: hādāk with ذ→/d/ (Algiers stop reflex); bzzāf 'a lot' (shared Maghrebi); yixdim 'works'; Algiers interdental→dental-stop merger (ذ→[d]) per (boucherit2002)

sentence: رُوح لِلسُّوق وْشْرِي الْخُبْز يَا خُويَا.
raw: روح للسوق وشري الخبز يا خويا.
ipa: ˈruːħ lisˈsuːq ˈwʃriː lˈxubz ˈjaː ˈxuːjaː
gloss_en: Go to the market and buy the bread, brother.
notes: Algiers imperatives ruh/shri with syncope clusters and xuya vocative (boucherit2002)

gloss_en: The weather isn't cold today.
notes: rāh(-š) progressive/negation particle with ma-…-š circumfix (boucherit2002); jaww ج=/ʒ/ sedentary Maghrebi (watson2002)

gloss_en: How much is this? Two hundred dinars.
notes: šḥāl 'how much' and mītīn 'two hundred' Maghrebi numeral (boucherit2002)

gloss_en: The appointment is at eight tomorrow morning.
notes: ث→/t/ in tmnija — Algiers interdental merger to stops (boucherit2002; marcais1902); sun assimilation aṣ-ṣ; ɣadwa 'tomorrow'

gloss_en: My family lives in the capital.
notes: ʕayla 'family' with tiskun 3fs; pharyngeal ع; emphatic ṣ backing /a/ in ʕāṣima (boucherit2002; watson2002)

gloss_en: Go straight and turn right.
notes: syncopated imperatives mši/dur with initial clusters (boucherit2002); nišan 'straight'

gloss_en: The food was very tasty.
notes: mākla 'food', bnina 'tasty' (fem) and bzzaf 'a lot' (boucherit2002); ك plain /k/ (grand_henry1972)

gloss_en: Where's the key? I can't find it.
notes: win 'where' and ma-nlqah-š negation (boucherit2002); ق=/q/ old-urban Algiers in nlqah (boucherit2002; grand_henry1972)

gloss_en: How many children do you have?
notes: šḥāl … men wlad partitive 'how many children' (boucherit2002); ك=/k/ in ʕandek

sentence: إِذَا جِيت غَدْوَة نْرُوحُو مَع بَعْضْنَا.
raw: إذا جيت غدوة نروحو مع بعضنا.
ipa: ˈʔidaː ˈʒiːt ˈɣadwa ˈnruːħuː ˈmaʕ ˈbaʕdˤnaː
gloss_en: If you come tomorrow, we'll go together.
notes: ذ→/d/ in ida — interdental merger (boucherit2002); ض→/dˤ/ in baʕdna; n- 1pl nruħu (marcais1902)

gloss_en: Yesterday I went to the market and bought vegetables.
notes: bareħ 'yesterday', šrit 'I bought', xuḍra 'vegetables' (boucherit2002); ق=/q/ in suq

gloss_en: Next week I'll travel to Oran.
notes: jumʕa 'week', jaya 'coming', nsafer with n- 1sg (boucherit2002); ج=/ʒ/ (watson2002)

sentence: عَيَّطْ لِي فِي اللِّيل يَا خُويَا.
raw: عيط لي في الليل يا خويا.
ipa: ˈʕajjɑtˤ ˈliː ˈfiː lˈliːl ˈjaː ˈxuːjaː
gloss_en: Call me tonight, brother.
notes: ʕayyaṭ 'to call' imperative (boucherit2002); emphasis backs /a/→[ɑ] near ṭ (watson2002); xuya vocative

sentence: صْبَاح الْخِير، وَاش رَاك؟ لَابَاس الْحَمْدُ لِلَّه.
raw: صباح الخير، واش راك؟ لاباس الحمد لله.
ipa: ˈsˤbaːħ alˈxiːr ˈwaːʃ ˈraːk laːˈbaːs alˈħamdu ˈlillah
gloss_en: Good morning, how are you? Fine, thank God.
notes: ṣbaħ l-xir greeting; wāš rāk deictic question (boucherit2002); labas 'fine'

sentence: الْيَد اللِّي مَا تِقْدِرْش تْعَضّهَا بُوسْهَا.
raw: اليد اللي ما تقدرش تعضها بوسها.
ipa: ˈaljad ˈalliː ˈmaː tiqˈdirʃ ˈtʕɑdˤdˤhaː ˈbuːshaː
gloss_en: The hand you can't bite, kiss it.
notes: proverb; ق=/q/ in teqder (boucherit2002; grand_henry1972); geminate ض in ʕeḍḍ; busha 'kiss it' imperative
